display.purpose: Page and program titles. Stenciled, letter-spaced, often with text-shadow for the stamped-on-paper feel.

headline.purpose: Section headers and workout-day titles inside bracketed cards.

title.purpose: Card subtitles, button labels, dialog titles.

body.purpose: Workout descriptions, instructional copy, paragraphs. Cap at 65–75ch in long-form.

label.purpose: Form labels, captions, badge text, nav labels.

shadows.name: button-press-rest
shadows.purpose: Primary button at rest — the gameboy press default.

shadows.name: button-press-hover
shadows.purpose: Primary button on :hover — adds an outer emerald glow without changing the press geometry.

shadows.name: button-press-active
shadows.purpose: Primary button on :active — paired with translateY(4px). The button presses in.

shadows.name: card-completed
shadows.purpose: Card with a completed workout. Outer emerald glow at 30% RGB opacity.

shadows.name: card-in-progress
shadows.purpose: Card with an in-progress workout. Pulses between 20px and 40px outer glow at 2s ease-in-out.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus on any interactive element. Triple-stack: background gap, primary ring, outer glow.

shadows.name: card-modal
shadows.purpose: Modal dialog panels. The only place a non-state ambient shadow is permitted, because the modal is itself a state.

motion.name: button-press-transition
motion.purpose: Primary button state transitions. Snappy — the press should feel immediate.

motion.name: card-state-transition
motion.purpose: Card border/shadow transitions when workout state changes.

motion.name: input-focus-transition
motion.purpose: Input border, background, and shadow on focus.

motion.name: doom-pulse
motion.purpose: In-progress workout card animated glow. Oscillates between 20px and 40px outer shadow.

motion.name: pwa-btn-shine
motion.purpose: The PWA install button's rare diagonal shimmer — paired with the rare-round corner treatment as eye-draw.

signatureUtilities.name: doom-corners
signatureUtilities.css: .ds-doom-corners { position: relative; } .ds-doom-corners::before { content: ''; position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 16px; height: 16px; border-top: 3px solid #10B981; border-left: 3px solid #10B981; z-index: 3; } .ds-doom-corners::after { content: ''; position: absolute; bottom: 0; right: 0; width: 16px; height: 16px; border-bottom: 3px solid #10B981; border-right: 3px solid #10B981; z-index: 3; }
signatureUtilities.purpose: The L-bracket card frame. Two 16×16px corner brackets in primary emerald, applied via ::before and ::after. The signature card chrome.

signatureUtilities.name: doom-noise
signatureUtilities.css: .ds-doom-noise { position: relative; } .ds-doom-noise::before { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; background-image: url("data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg viewBox='0 0 256 256' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%3E%3Cfilter id='noise'%3E%3CfeTurbulence type='fractalNoise' baseFrequency='0.9' numOctaves='4' /%3E%3C/filter%3E%3Crect width='100%25' height='100%25' filter='url(%23noise)' opacity='0.03'/%3E%3C/svg%3E"); pointer-events: none; z-index: 1; border-radius: inherit; } .ds-doom-noise > * { position: relative; z-index: 2; }
signatureUtilities.purpose: Low-opacity SVG fractal noise overlay (~3% opacity) for paper-grain texture. Apply to hero panels and modal surfaces.

signatureUtilities.name: doom-angular
signatureUtilities.css: .ds-doom-angular { border-radius: 0; clip-path: polygon(0% 8px, 8px 0%, calc(100% - 8px) 0%, 100% 8px, 100% calc(100% - 8px), calc(100% - 8px) 100%, 8px 100%, 0% calc(100% - 8px)); }
signatureUtilities.purpose: Octagonal chamfered-corner shape (8px corner-cut). An alternative to doom-corners when the bracket pseudo-elements would conflict.

components.name: Primary Button (Gameboy Press)
components.description: The default primary action. Hard 0-radius, gameboy-press 3D shadow that collapses on :active.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: Rajdhani, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #FFFFFF; background: #10B981; border: none; padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.1s ease; box-shadow: 0 4px 0 #047857, 0 6px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.25), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.2); transform: translateY(0); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #059669; box-shadow: 0 4px 0 #047857, 0 0 30px rgba(16,185,129,0.6), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.3); } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #047857; transform: translateY(4px); box-shadow: 0 0 0 #047857, 0 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.4), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.2); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #FBF8F3, 0 0 0 6px #10B981, 0 4px 0 #047857; }

components.name: Rare-Rounded CTA
components.description: The exception button. Rounded 12px, banana-gold fill, with a slow shimmer. Used at most once per screen as eye-draw.
components.css: .ds-btn-rare-rounded { position: relative; overflow: hidden; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; font-family: Rajdhani, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #3A2817; background: #F59E0B; border: none; padding: 16px 32px; border-radius: 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.2s ease; box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(245,158,11,0.3); } .ds-btn-rare-rounded::before { content: ''; position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; background: linear-gradient(105deg, transparent 30%, rgba(255,255,255,0.25) 45%, rgba(255,255,255,0.35) 50%, rgba(255,255,255,0.25) 55%, transparent 70%); transform: translateX(-100%); animation: ds-shine 8s ease-in-out 2s infinite; pointer-events: none; } .ds-btn-rare-rounded:hover { background: #D97706; box-shadow: 0 6px 16px rgba(245,158,11,0.5); } .ds-btn-rare-rounded:active { transform: translateY(1px); } @keyframes ds-shine { 0%, 85% { transform: translateX(-100%); } 100% { transform: translateX(100%); } }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Non-primary inline action. Warm-tan background, flat (no 3D press), hard 0-radius.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { font-family: Rajdhani, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #3A2817; background: #F5EFE6; border: 2px solid #D4C4B0; padding: 10px 22px; border-radius: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #E8DCC8; border-color: #A88C6F; } .ds-btn-secondary:active { background: #D4C4B0; }

components.name: Bracketed Card
components.description: The signature card frame. Card-cream surface, sandy-brown border, and emerald L-bracket pseudo-elements in opposing corners.
components.html:
DAY 1: MACHINE WORKOUT
5 exercises·May 13
components.css: .ds-card-bracketed { position: relative; background: #FFFCF7; color: #3A2817; border: 1px solid #D4C4B0; border-radius: 0; padding: 16px 20px; transition: all 0.3s ease; } .ds-card-bracketed::before { content: ''; position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 16px; height: 16px; border-top: 3px solid #10B981; border-left: 3px solid #10B981; z-index: 3; } .ds-card-bracketed::after { content: ''; position: absolute; bottom: 0; right: 0; width: 16px; height: 16px; border-bottom: 3px solid #10B981; border-right: 3px solid #10B981; z-index: 3; } .ds-card-bracketed:hover { border-color: rgba(212,147,10,0.4); } .ds-card-title { font-family: Rajdhani, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.125rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; margin-bottom: 6px; } .ds-card-meta { font-family: Rajdhani, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #78573B; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Form input. Warm-tan fill, 2px sandy-brown border, hard 0-radius. Focus shifts border to emerald + outer glow.
components.css: .ds-input-stamped { font-family: Rajdhani, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: #3A2817; background: #F5EFE6; border: 2px solid #D4C4B0; border-radius: 0; padding: 12px 16px; width: 100%; transition: all 0.3s ease; } .ds-input-stamped::placeholder { color: #78573B; text-transform: uppercase; font-size: 0.875rem; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } .ds-input-stamped:focus { outline: none; border-color: #10B981; background: #FFFCF7; box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(16,185,129,0.3); }

components.name: Status Badge (Clipped Chip)
components.description: Status pill with subtle parallelogram chamfer via clip-path. Solid state color + uppercase label.
components.html: COMPLETED
components.css: .ds-badge-completed { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 4px 12px; font-family: Rajdhani, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: #FFFFFF; background: #10B981; border-radius: 0; clip-path: polygon(4px 0%, 100% 0%, calc(100% - 4px) 100%, 0% 100%); box-shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(16,185,129,0.5); }

components.name: Bottom Navigation
components.description: Mobile-first fixed bottom nav with four tabs. Active tab shifts to banana-gold accent with a 3px golden underline.
components.html: TrainingProgramsLearnSettings
components.css: .ds-bottom-nav { display: flex; align-items: stretch; height: 56px; background: rgba(245,239,230,0.95); border-top: 2px solid #D4C4B0; backdrop-filter: blur(8px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(8px); } .ds-bottom-nav-tab { flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 2px; color: #78573B; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.2s ease; font-family: Rajdhani, sans-serif; } .ds-bottom-nav-tab span { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-bottom-nav-tab:hover { color: #3A2817; } .ds-bottom-nav-tab--active { color: #D4930A; border-bottom: 3px solid #FBBF24; }

narrative.overview:
Ripit is styled as a warm paper field guide a grown-up would reach for to learn a new physical skill, with cartridge-era (NES → SNES → GBA) detailing carried in the geometry, textures, and tactility. The page background is warm cream with dark-brown body text. Cards sit as flat parchment panels with sharp L-bracket corners. Buttons feel like physical printed buttons that press 4px when you tap them. Headings are uppercase stenciled Rajdhani with positive letter-spacing. The frog mascot is the guide's illustrator's signature — present on the splash and at empty states, never decorating the workout data itself.

The system explicitly refuses three lanes documented in PRODUCT.md: gym-bro / influencer-culture aesthetics, generic clean-white SaaS, and stock illustration / beauty-shot photography.

Ripit ships twelve user-selectable themes as a first-class personalization feature — a deliberate point of difference from every other fitness app in the category. The palette documented here is the canonical ripit default (Donkey-Kong jungle); eleven alternates ship as opt-in (doom, catppuccin, cyber, forest, synthwave, dracula, github, clyde, ninety, blossom, okabe). Every theme implements the same CSS-custom-property role schema, so every component and rule in this spec applies theme-agnostically. The roles are stable; only the hex values rotate.

The single load-bearing tension is friendly enough for a gym newcomer + serious enough for an adult who's never trained. We solve it by keeping the playful retro language in the chrome around the data and keeping the data itself — sets, reps, weights, history — plain, legible, and trustworthy.
keyCharacteristics: Warm cream page with rich-brown text — never #fff, never #000
Emerald jungle-green primary, banana gold accent, barrel brown secondary, DK-tie red error
Rajdhani everywhere — uppercase with positive letter-spacing for headings/labels, sentence-case for body
Sharp 0-radius corners by default; rounded corners are a deliberate exception, used as an eye-draw signal
Cards framed by doom-corners L-brackets in primary green
Buttons press with doom-button-3d — 4px hard shadow that translates on :active
Glow and animated pulse reserved for state changes, never decorative
Real photos of real lifters in real gyms — no stock illustration, no studio lighting
Twelve user-selectable themes implementing the same role schema — ripit is canonical, eleven alternates ship for personalization

rules.name: The Field-Guide-Not-the-Game Rule
rules.body: Retro language lives in the geometry, textures, and chrome. The data (sets, reps, weights, history) stays plain, legible, and serious. If a screen reads as 'playing a game' rather than 'consulting a manual about training,' roll it back.

rules.name: The Adult-Newcomer Rule
rules.body: Two readers have to feel at home: a gym newcomer who's never trained, and an older adult new to the app. Anything that reads as 'for kids' or 'for gamers' — chunky pixel fonts in the data layer, cartoon mascots inside workout cards, scoreboard-style copy — has failed the test. The frog is allowed on splashes, empty states, and the loading screen; not inside the workout itself.

rules.name: The Role-Stability Rule
rules.body: Component code, named rules, and screen designs target token roles (--primary, --background, --accent, etc.), never specific hex values. Hard-coding #10B981 into a component is forbidden; reference var(--color-primary) or the corresponding Tailwind utility (bg-primary, text-foreground) so the component renders correctly across all twelve themes. New themes added later inherit the entire component library for free.

rules.name: The Warm-Neutrals Rule
rules.body: In the canonical ripit palette: no #fff, no #000, no carbon-gray. Every neutral carries warm-brown chroma. The page is cream, the card is cream, the text is brown, the border is sandy. Cold neutrals belong to opt-in themes (doom, cyber, dracula, etc.), not to the canonical ripit look.

rules.name: The 10% Primary Rule
rules.body: The primary role carries roughly 5–15% of any given screen, regardless of theme. It marks actions and active state; it never fills large surface areas. A screen filled with primary has failed the test — whether that primary is ripit's Jungle Emerald, doom's Hell-Fire Orange, or blossom's Hot Pink.

rules.name: The Rarity-Is-The-Point Rule
rules.body: The accent role appears on ≤5% of any screen and only on elements meant to attract the eye (rare round-cornered CTAs, active-tab underlines). Saturate it across cards or borders and the eye-draw signal collapses — theme-agnostic.

rules.name: The UPPERCASE-Stenciled-Headings Rule
rules.body: Display, headline, title, and label styles are uppercase with positive letter-spacing (0.05–0.1em). Body and prose stay sentence case. Mixing them produces flat hierarchy; respecting them produces the field-guide voice.

rules.name: The Tabular-Numerics Rule
rules.body: Any number the user reads to act on (weights, reps, sets, durations, history) renders with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. Visual alignment of stacked numbers is part of the trust contract.

rules.name: The No-Decorative-Type Rule
rules.body: No gradient text. No outlined letters. No animated character reveals on workout data. Rajdhani's own weight and tracking carries all the expression we need.

rules.name: The Flat-Until-State Rule
rules.body: Cards and surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows and glows arrive only as a response to state — hover, focus, in-progress, completed. No ambient drop-shadows on resting surfaces. Decorative shadow is forbidden.

rules.name: The Press-Means-Press Rule
rules.body: Primary buttons must translateY(4px) on :active and collapse their offset shadow to zero. The tactile feedback is load-bearing — it's how a button reads as a physical thing rather than a styled div. Skipping this treatment on a primary button is a regression.

dos: Do default to sharp 0-radius corners on every element. Cards, buttons, inputs, modals, badges — hard edges are the system's default voice.

Do use rounded corners as a rare eye-draw signal — primarily the PWA install button, the occasional end-of-flow CTA, and indicator pills meant to interrupt the visual rhythm. Their incongruence is the signal.
Do apply doom-corners L-brackets to addressable card regions in the primary emerald. The frame is the brand.
Do keep numbers in font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums so logged sets stack cleanly.
Do translate primary buttons 4px on :active and collapse their offset shadow. The press is load-bearing.
Do keep the warm-brown chroma in every neutral (#FBF8F3 cream, #3A2817 brown, #D4C4B0 sandy border) — even at scale.
Do use real photos of real lifters in real gyms for exercise demonstrations.
Do reserve glow and pulse animations for state changes — completed, in-progress, focused, just-logged — never decoratively on resting surfaces.
Do use UPPERCASE Rajdhani with 0.05–0.1em letter-spacing on every title, label, and button. Reserve sentence case for body and prose.
Do preserve the OKABE color-blind-safe theme as a peer of the default — never an afterthought.
Do reference token roles (var(--color-primary), bg-primary, text-foreground) instead of hex values in component code, so every component works across all twelve themes.
donts: Don't use macho-neon palettes, carbon-fiber backgrounds, shirtless-influencer photography, or 'BEAST MODE' copy. The system explicitly rejects gym-bro and influencer-culture aesthetics.

Don't make Ripit look like Apple Fitness, Centr, or Peloton's app — generic clean-white SaaS with thin sans on #fff, blue accents, abstract gradients, and rounded-lg everywhere is the second lane we refuse.
Don't use stock illustration, mascots-doing-yoga vectors, or studio-lit beauty photography for exercise demos. Real people, real gyms, or nothing.
Don't use pure #000 or #fff anywhere. The neutrals carry warm-brown chroma.
Don't put rounded corners on cards, inputs, or workout-list items. Round is reserved.
Don't put gradient text on headings. Rajdhani's weight and tracking carries all the expression we need.

Don't use side-stripe border-left or border-right accents greater than 1px as a colored stripe on cards or list items as decoration. The 4px secondary-brown strip on the current workout card is the only allowed use; everywhere else it's banned.
Don't apply ambient drop-shadows to cards at rest. Shadows arrive with state.
Don't drop the frog mascot inside workout data. The frog belongs on splash, empty states, and the loading screen. Never on a logged set.
Don't game-ify the data layer — no XP bars, no streak fireworks, no scoreboard styling on weights. The Field-Guide-Not-the-Game Rule governs every screen.
Don't use em dashes (—) in UI copy. Use commas, colons, semicolons, or periods. (And not -- either.)
Don't mock up new components in rounded-lg 'because it looks clean.' Hard edges are the default; cleanness is not the brand goal.
Don't hard-code hex values into components. Reference token roles instead. Hard-coded hex breaks the twelve-theme contract.
